tagline: Season One — six stories from examples to judgment

units.title: Training Data
units.subtitle: How AI learns from examples
units.description: Rex and Dino discover that a smart trail only understands the examples it has seen.

units.title: Data Quality
units.subtitle: When examples go wrong
units.description: A clever berry basket learns the wrong lesson when examples become rotten, fake, old, or mislabeled.

units.title: AI Bias
units.subtitle: Unfair patterns in the data
units.description: A polite mirror seems smart, but its recommendations become unfair because the examples are unbalanced.

units.title: AI Hallucination
units.subtitle: Confidence is not evidence
units.description: Echo Cave answers with confidence, but Rex and Dino learn that sounding sure is not the same as being right.

units.title: Model Collapse
units.subtitle: When copies drift from truth
units.description: Copied maps slowly lose contact with the real forest, showing how repeated copies can drift from truth.

units.title: Human Judgment
units.subtitle: Why people still matter
units.description: Atlas Tortoise checks the fastest path against real conditions, proving that useful tools still need people.
